title: Solid conditioner bar
subtitle: A shea butter, coconut oil, and beeswax bar that melts on the hair shaft to condition
excerpt: Rub the bar between wet palms to emulsify, then work through mid-lengths and ends. Shea butter and coconut oil add softness and shine; beeswax gives the bar enough firmness to hold its shape at room temperature. One bar lasts as long as two to three bottles of liquid conditioner.
sourceNotes: Beeswax as a solid-bar former for hair products: cosmetic formulation reference. Coconut oil and hair shaft penetration: Rele and Mohile, Journal of Cosmetic Science (2003). Shea butter emollient properties: Akihisa et al., Journal of Oleo Science (2010).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Use the silicone loaf mould and slice into bars once set.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: These bars keep for a year at room temperature. Make a batch and wrap individually in wax paper.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within one year. Keep on a draining soap dish between uses to prevent softening. Discard if the bar develops a rancid smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: Rendered and pelletised beeswax. The solid structure in a conditioner bar -- it melts at body temperature on skin contact, releasing the conditioning oils. A higher beeswax ratio gives a firmer bar that lasts longer.

content.text: A solid conditioner bar works differently from a bottle of liquid conditioner. Rather than rinsing quickly through the hair, the oil-heavy formula clings to the cuticle and requires proper emulsification on the palm before application.
provide the solid structure that allows the bar to keep its shape while still melting smoothly on warm, wet palms. Shea butter and coconut oil are the conditioning actives.

items.prepNote: about 0.5 ml; omit in first trimester

content.text: Melt the beeswax pellets in a double boiler until fully liquid. Add the shea butter and coconut oil and stir until fully melted and combined. Remove from the heat and cool to 50 to 55°C.

Add the essential oils and stir through. Pour immediately into the silicone mould. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for at least one hour -- or refrigerate for 20 minutes to speed setting. Once set, pop out and slice into individual bar-sized pieces with a knife.

After shampooing, hold the bar in both hands and work it between the palms under warm running water until the oils begin to melt and emulsify into a thin film. Apply this film from mid-lengths to the ends of the hair, working through with fingers. Leave for 1 to 2 minutes, then rinse out thoroughly. Using too much leaves the hair feeling heavy -- one pass of the bar between the palms is usually enough.

Store on a draining soap dish to let the bar dry between uses. The beeswax means the bar is quite heat-resistant, but store away from direct sun and hot surfaces. Wrap unused bars in wax paper and keep at room temperature for up to one year.
